content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "project-intelligence",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 72,
    "domain_depth": 58,
    "data_richness": 45,
    "interaction_quality": 68,
    "ai_integration": 75,
    "mobile_first": 42,
    "performance": 60,
    "typescript_quality": 65,
    "error_handling": 55,
    "realtime": 40,
    "accessibility": 35,
    "security": 52,
    "differentiation": 70,
    "enterprise_readiness": 48
  },
  "overall_score": 56,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "CopilotPanel chat input and agent menu lack ARIA attributes",
      "file": "src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx",
      "description": "The copilot slide-in panel has no role='dialog' or aria-label. The agent menu dropdown has no role='listbox'. Chat messages have no aria-live region for screen reader announcements of new messages. Keyboard users cannot navigate agent suggestions.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: Add role='dialog' and aria-label='AI Copilot' to the outermost motion.aside element. Wrap the messages scroll container with aria-live='polite' and aria-relevant='additions'. BEFORE: The panel aside has no ARIA role or label; messages container has no live region. AFTER: The aside has role='dialog' aria-label='AI Copilot'; the messages div has aria-live='polite' aria-relevant='additions'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "FloatingAIButton silently fails when insights query errors with no retry UI",
      "file": "src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx",
      "description": "When useAIInsights returns isError, the button shows a small warning triangle but no toast notification or accessible error text. The refetch call on click has no error handling — if refetch also fails, nothing happens. Users on flaky field connections get no feedback.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component: Add error handling to the onClick callback so that when isError is true and refetch is called, catch any thrown error and display a toast via toast.error('Unable to load AI insights. Please try again.'). Also add an aria-label to the warning triangle span: aria-label='AI insights failed to load'. BEFORE: The onClick calls refetch() and openCopilot() without catching errors; the warning triangle span has no accessible label. AFTER: The onClick wraps refetch() in a try/catch that calls toast.error on failure; the warning span has aria-label='AI insights failed to load'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "CopilotPanel is fixed 360px wide with no mobile responsive breakpoint",
      "file": "src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx",
      "description": "The panel is hardcoded to width 360px with maxWidth 85vw. On mobile (375px), this leaves only 15% of the screen visible behind the scrim. The panel should be full-width on screens under 640px. Touch targets for suggested prompts and agent mention items are below the 44px minimum.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: Change the motion.aside width from the hardcoded '360px' to use a CSS calc or conditional: on screens under 640px, use width '100%' instead of '360px'. Since this uses inline styles, add a useState that checks window.innerWidth on mount and on resize (with cleanup). If width < 640, set panelWidth to '100vw'; otherwise '360px'. Also set maxWidth to '100vw' on mobile. BEFORE: width is always '360px', maxWidth '85vw'. AFTER: width is '100vw' on screens < 640px, '360px' otherwise; maxWidth is '100vw' on mobile. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "AgentMentionInput textarea does not auto-resize and has no character count indicator",
      "file": "src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx",
      "description": "The textarea has a fixed height. When users type long messages approaching the 4000 char limit, they cannot see how close they are. The toast fires once but provides no persistent visual feedback. The textarea should grow with content (up to a max) and show remaining characters when over 3500.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx, in the AgentMentionInput component: After the handleChange callback, add a useEffect that auto-resizes the textarea by setting scrollHeight on the ref (inputRef.current.style.height = 'auto'; inputRef.current.style.height = Math.min(inputRef.current.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px'). Add a character count indicator below the textarea that only renders when value.length > 3500, showing `${MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH - value.length} characters remaining` in colors.textTertiary at fontSize caption. BEFORE: Textarea is fixed height; no character count shown. AFTER: Textarea auto-grows up to 160px; character count appears when > 3500 chars typed. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "SuggestedPrompts returns generic prompts with no project-specific context",
      "file": "src/components/ai/SuggestedPrompts.tsx",
      "description": "The pageContextPrompts map is entirely hardcoded strings with no dynamic data. Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA for the AI Copilot, suggested prompts should be based on current page context. The component receives prompts as props but the parent never passes project-specific data like 'You have 5 overdue RFIs' into prompt text.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/SuggestedPrompts.tsx, in the SuggestedPrompts component: Add an optional prop contextData of type { overdueRfis?: number; openPunchItems?: number; budgetVariance?: string; daysToMilestone?: number }. When contextData is provided and has overdueRfis > 0, prepend a dynamic prompt to the list: `Review ${contextData.overdueRfis} overdue RFIs and suggest follow up actions`. Similarly for openPunchItems > 0: `Analyze ${contextData.openPunchItems} open punch items by trade`. Render these dynamic prompts first, before the static ones from pageContextPrompts. BEFORE: Only static hardcoded prompts are shown regardless of project state. AFTER: When contextData is provided, dynamic project-aware prompts appear first. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "The AI Copilot implementation significantly exceeds Procore's bolt-on chatbot with multi-agent orchestration, generative UI blocks, and domain-specific specialist agents. This is closer to what Autodesk Construction IQ aspires to. However, Procore Assist now answers project questions from real data — SiteSync's copilot still relies heavily on mock/fallback data. The generative UI system (charts, approval cards, schedule cards, cost breakdowns) is a genuine differentiator no competitor ships today. The gap is in reliability: field users on spotty connections need bulletproof error handling and offline queuing for AI queries, which is missing.",
  "summary": "The Project Intelligence module has an impressively ambitious architecture — multi-agent orchestration, generative UI rendering, specialist domain agents, and a rich component library. However, it is hampered by five surgical issues: the copilot panel lacks critical ARIA attributes making it inaccessible to keyboard and screen reader users; the floating AI button silently swallows errors with no user feedback; the panel is not mobile-responsive (critical for a field-first app); the input textarea has no auto-resize or character count feedback; and suggested prompts are entirely static with no project-aware context despite having the data infrastructure to support it. Fixing these five issues would lift the score from 56 to approximately 68 by addressing the weakest dimensions (accessibility 35→55, mobile 42→55, error handling 55→68, interaction quality 68→75, data richness 45→55)."
}
```
